query: Blue Origin Blue Ring Optimum Technologies Caracal optical payload first mission agreement 2026

mako.raw:
{"query":"Blue Origin Blue Ring Optimum Technologies Caracal optical payload first mission agreement 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1867,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.blueorigin.com/news/blue-ring-optimum-technologies-sensor","title":"Blue Ring to Become First Fully Commercial Space Domain Awareness Mission in GEO with Optimum Technologies’ Sensor | Blue Origin","content":"Blue Origin announced today an agreement with Optimum Technologies (OpTech) to integrate its next-generation Caracal optical payload onboard the first mission of Blue Ring, the highly maneuverable multi-mission spacecraft for payload delivery, hosting, and infrastructure services.
Blue Ring's first mission is expected to launch in 2026 with initial injection into Geostationary Transfer Orbit (GTO) and additional services performed in Geostationary Orbit (GEO).
The vehicle’s architecture promotes mission-unique adaptation to meet a variety of customer and internal mission needs in GEO, Cislunar, Mars, and beyond.
“By leveraging the already matured hardware and architecture, we’re able to rapidly field a low-cost, intelligent optical system on Blue Ring, extending mission value and new capabilities into GEO.”
Blue Origin today announced it will pause its New Shepard flights and shift resources to further accelerate development of the company's human lunar capabilities.
Blue Origin announced that New Glenn’s next mission will carry AST SpaceMobile’s next-generation Block 2 BlueBird satellite to low Earth orbit.
On January 22, 2026, Blue Origin successfully completed the 38th flight for the New Shepard program and first of 2026."},{"url":"https://www.satellitetoday.com/technology/2025/11/26/blue-origin-to-integrate-optical-payload-on-blue-ring-vehicle-for-space-domain-awareness-mission/","title":"Blue Origin to Integrate Optical Payload on Blue Ring Vehicle for Space Domain Awareness Mission - Via Satellite","content":"Blue Origin will integrate an optical payload from Optimum Technologies (OpTech) on the first operational Blue Ring mission, set to launch in 2026.
The vehicle will perform a space domain awareness (SDA) mission, including object tracking and characterization in Geostationary Orbit (GEO)."},{"url":"https://satnews.com/2025/11/24/blue-origins-blue-ring-will-be-1st-commercial-geo-sda-mission-with-optimum-technologies-sensor/","title":"Blue Origin’s Blue Ring will be 1st commercial GEO SDA mission with Optimum Technologies’ sensor – SatNews","content":"Blue Origin’s Blue Ring will be 1st commercial GEO SDA mission with Optimum Technologies’ sensor – SatNews
Space Force’s Tactically Responsive Space program, which is currently in hardware build with scheduled space vehicle integration and assembly in spring 2026.
Caracal’s telescope and imaging components for the VICTUS SURGO mission will achieve “flight qualified” status after assembly and testing."},{"url":"https://orbitaltoday.com/2025/12/02/blue-origins-new-blue-ring-to-carry-caracal-on-first-flight/","title":"Blue Origin's New Blue Ring To Carry Caracal On First Flight - Orbital Today","content":"The US-based space technology company Blue Origin will integrate the Caracal optical payload of Optimum Technologies (OpTech) on the maiden flight of its spacecraft Blue Ring slated for 2026.
Caracal will fly with Scout Space’s Owl sensor, along with internally developed payloads.
Blue Origin said its spacecraft has industry-leading capabilities, including a nominal Delta-V of at least 3,000m/s (with a max of 4,000m/s) -capacity to change the spacecraft’s speed-enabled by electric and chemical propulsion systems.
In 2024 OpTech was awarded a $4.5 million contract by the United States Space Force (USSF)’s Space Systems Command (SSC)’s Space Safari Office to provide an optical imaging payload, and surrounding avionics and software, for the Tactically Responsive Space (TacRS) mission, VICTUS SURGO, projected for launch in 2026.
The contract, awarded on 28 October 2024 under a Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Phase 3 agreement, is funded by the US Space Force."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/blue-origin_we-look-forward-to-integrating-optimum-technologies-activity-7398792275427950592-NGkB","title":"We look forward to integrating Optimum Technologies LLC's next-generation Caracal optical payload onboard the first mission of Blue Ring: https://bit.ly/4imEwu0 | Blue Origin | 12 comments","content":"Integrating optical payloads like Caracal into Blue Origin Blue Ring platform shows how fast space borne technology is advancing."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Blue Origin Blue Ring Optimum Technologies Caracal optical payload first mission agreement 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.blueorigin.com/news/blue-ring-optimum-technologies-sensor","title":"Blue Ring to Become First Fully Commercial Space Domain Awareness Mission in GEO with Optimum Technologies’ Sensor | Blue Origin","content":"# Blue Ring to Become First Fully Commercial Space Domain Awareness Mission in GEO with Optimum Technologies’ Sensor. Blue Origin announced today an agreement with Optimum Technologies (OpTech) to integrate its next-generation Caracal optical payload onboard the first mission of Blue Ring, the highly maneuverable multi-mission spacecraft for payload delivery, hosting, and infrastructure services. Caracal will fly with Scout Space’s Owl sensor, along with internally developed payloads, all demonstrating Blue Ring as the ideal platform for supporting future GEO space domain awareness missions. \"We’re looking forward to integrating Caracal onboard Blue Ring’s first mission and utilizing our vehicle to advance our customers’ critical missions,” said Paul Ebertz, senior vice president of Blue Origin’s In-Space Systems business unit. The effort represents a follow-on to OpTech’s contract awarded in 2024 to develop Caracal for the U.S. Space Force’s Tactically Responsive Space program, which is currently in hardware build with scheduled space vehicle integration and assembly in spring 2026.","score":0.9203972,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://orbitaltoday.com/2025/12/02/blue-origins-new-blue-ring-to-carry-caracal-on-first-flight","title":"Blue Origin's New Blue Ring To Carry Caracal On First Flight","content":"Blue Origin will integrate the Caracal optical payload of Optimum Technologies on the maiden flight of its spacecraft Blue Ring in 2026.","score":0.87706697,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/photo.php?fbid=885407130674011&set=a.164363109445087&id=100076141962984","title":"Space - Blue Origin just announced they're teaming up with Optimum ...","content":"...
